sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Follow-up of chronic problems, including depression, type 2 diabetes, and hypertension.
History of Present Illness
Bruce is a 60-year-old male with a past medical history of depression, type 2 diabetes, and hypertension. He presents for follow-up. He reports experiencing anxiety and pressure related to Thanksgiving in the past year and a half, which he managed by taking long walks outside and doing yard work during the summer. However, with the changing seasons and colder weather, he has found it difficult to maintain these activities and is seeking alternative options. He reports difficulty maintaining a diet during the holidays. He is currently taking metformin 1000mg twice daily and lisinopril. He denies current chest pain, shortness of breath, or belly pain, and reports no weight loss. He reports no blood in stool.
Findings
Physical examination reveals a slight two out of six systolic ejection murmur heard at the left base, grade 1, and one plus non-pitting edema bilaterally in the lower extremities. An EKG shows no immediate concerns. Laboratory results demonstrate an elevated hemoglobin A1c of 8.2. The nurse performed an EKG prior to the patient's arrival, which showed no immediate concerns.
Assessment

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The patient's primary problems include depression, type 2 diabetes, and hypertension. He is managing his depression with alternative options and requires further evaluation and support.
Plan
I will refer the patient to psychiatry for talk therapy to address his depression. I will order a hemoglobin A1c test in the next two months. I will increase the metformin dosage to 1000mg twice daily. I will order a lipid panel. I will order an echocardiogram to re-evaluate the heart murmur. The nurse will schedule follow-up appointments and provide patient education regarding medication adherence and lifestyle modifications.
